refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation period clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime to support teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Monitor and report link availability status to stakeholders.

candidate_dps: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms
Real-time link health monitoring and automatic recovery protocols
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability management from other system functions, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring DP in next decomposition level to enhance detection and recovery.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: All hardware and software components responsible for establishing, maintaining, and managing the teleoperation network link.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send link status and availability metrics to the NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands dependent on link availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of link availability functions; low diffusion risk.
Limited containment; some diffusion due to embedded network interfaces.
Minimal containment; indirect involvement via safety alerts.
Moderate containment; potential diffusion if link management is spread.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management within the Teleoperation module minimizes interface complexity and diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle embedded network components causing interface ambiguity.
Risk of insufficient coordination with Safety Services for safety-critical link failures.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface boundaries with Vehicle and Safety Services to reduce diffusion risk.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear performance targets and logical independence.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules due to embedded network hardware.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment of link availability functions, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring link availability metrics via Teleoperation interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface contracts between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules for network hardware.
Engage Safety Services early to integrate safety-critical link failure alerts.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, interface clarity and safety integration require refinement to mitigate diffusion and safety risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime to support teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Monitor and report link availability status to stakeholders.

revise.why: To clarify interface boundaries and ownership overlap with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Teleoperation module can encapsulate link availability management.
open_risks: Interface ambiguity between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
Safety-critical link failure alerts integration with Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Interface specifications between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
Safety alert protocols for link failures.
